text: Which of the following describes the 'Scaling Policy' of a multi-cluster warehouse? (Choose two.)

options.a: Standard: Starts clusters immediately to minimize queuing.
options.b: Economy: Favors credit saving by only starting clusters if the system estimates there is enough load to keep it busy for 6 minutes.
options.c: Performance: Resizes the warehouse size (e.g., Small to Large) automatically.

text: True or False: A Snowflake 'Share' can be used to share data from a Database created from another Share (Nested Sharing).
What is the primary function of the 'Result Cache'?

options.a: To store the data files of a table.
options.b: To hold the results of a query for 24 hours so that the same query can be returned instantly without using a warehouse.
options.c: To manage the metadata of a warehouse.

text: Which role is required to view the 'Account Usage' views in the Snowflake database?

What happens when you increase the size of a Warehouse while it is currently running queries?

options.a: The queries are killed and restarted.
options.b: The existing queries finish on the old size, and new queries start on the new size.

text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Stages' in a schema?
True or False: Snowflake 'Snowpipe' requires a Virtual Warehouse to be actively running to ingest data.
Which of the following is a benefit of 'Zero-copy cloning'?

options.a: It reduces the amount of metadata.
options.b: It allows for rapid creation of development and test environments without duplicating data storage costs.

text: What does the 'ON_ERROR = SKIP_FILE' option do in a COPY INTO command?

options.a: It stops the load at the first error.
options.b: It ignores the file that has an error and continues loading the other files.

text: True or False: Snowflake supports 'Multi-factor Authentication' (MFA) for the SnowSQL CLI tool.
Which command is used to see the DDL of a 'Task'?
What is the primary purpose of 'Object Tagging'?

options.b: To group and track Snowflake objects for sensitive data tracking and cost management.
options.d: To share data across cloud providers.

text: Which role is the only one that can manage 'OrgAdmin' privileges by default?
True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Row Access Policies' on Materialized Views.

What is 'Snowsight'?

options.a: The command line interface for Snowflake.
options.b: The modern web interface for Snowflake that includes worksheets, dashboards, and data exploration.

text: Which command is used to remove a file from an Internal Stage?
True or False: A 'Permanent' table always has a 7-day 'Fail-safe' period.
Which Snowflake component handles 'Query Parsing' and 'Compilation'?
What does 'Scale Up' mean in the context of a Virtual Warehouse?

options.a: Adding more clusters to handle more users.
options.b: Increasing the T-shirt size (e.g., Small to Medium) to handle larger/more complex queries.
options.c: Adding more disks to the storage layer.
options.d: Moving the account to a higher edition.

text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Users' in an account?

True or False: A 'Transient' table survives a system crash.
Which role is primarily responsible for creating and managing 'Resource Monitors'?
What is the result of 'Scaling Out' a multi-cluster warehouse?

options.a: Queries run on a larger cluster.
options.b: Additional clusters of the same size are started to handle high concurrency.
options.d: Billing moves to a monthly model.

text: True or False: Snowflake handles the 'Rotation of encryption keys' automatically.
Which command is used to add a 'Clustering Key' to a table?
What is the primary function of a 'Task' in Snowflake?

options.a: To ingest data from a stage.
options.b: To execute a single SQL statement or stored procedure on a recurring schedule.

text: Which role is the only one that can see 'Organization' level billing across multiple accounts?
True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'External Functions' to call an external API hosted on Azure Functions.
What is 'Pruning' in Snowflake?

options.b: The process where Snowflake skips reading micro-partitions that it knows do not contain data relevant to the query filters.
options.c: Reducing the size of a warehouse.

options.d: It is moved to a backup database.

What is the purpose of 'Time Travel'?

options.b: To access and restore data that has been modified or deleted over a specified window.
options.c: To archive data to long-term storage.

options.b: Increasing the T-shirt size (e.g., from Small to Medium).

options.b: To set limits on credit consumption and notify admins or suspend warehouses.

options.b: To obscure sensitive data in query results based on the user's role/permissions.
